fear: The coded letter he wrote to Hartwell — if it is found and deciphered, he is finished.
name: Thomas Dillworth
role: Merchant and peripheral member of the Sons of Liberty inner circle
voice: Smooth, measured, slightly over-helpful — he volunteers just enough information to seem cooperative while steering attention elsewhere; his sentences trail into reassuring generalities.
secrets: He received twenty pounds sterling from Hartwell hidden inside a bolt of cloth delivered to his counting house on King Street.
He wrote a coded letter to Hartwell using a cipher taught to him during a private meeting at the Custom House — the letter is hidden beneath a loose board in his counting house.
knowledge: He knows the exact timing and boarding plan for tonight's action — he provided it to Hartwell two days ago.
He knows that Hartwell has already dispatched a message to Admiral Montagu's flagship, but the response has not yet arrived.
publicFace: A prosperous, well-spoken merchant who frames his patriotism in economic terms — he opposes the tea tax because it harms his business, which makes him seem reasonable and relatable.
represents: REQUIRES FACT-CHECK — verify whether this is a documented historical individual from colonial Boston, 1773-1775
trustLogic: He is most forthcoming when he believes the player suspects someone else — he will subtly reinforce suspicion toward Elias Croft or Dr. Warren. He becomes evasive the moment specific dates or documents are mentioned.
introAnchor: a prosperous merchant and peripheral Sons of Liberty member whose patriotism is framed entirely in the language of commerce
privateGoal: Collect his final payment from Customs Officer Hartwell and disappear to Halifax before anyone connects the dots.

aggressionProfile.fleeStyle: Bolts toward the Custom House, weaving through the crowd on King Street, heading for Hartwell's protection.
aggressionProfile.chaseStyle: Knocks over barrels and shoves bystanders to slow pursuit; knows the back alleys between King Street and the waterfront.
aggressionProfile.mildPressure: Smiles warmly and redirects: 'A fair question — though I wonder if we shouldn't be looking more closely at those who joined us most recently.'
aggressionProfile.breakingPoint: He will never voluntarily admit the coded letter exists — he will claim any such document is a forgery or misunderstanding.
aggressionProfile.fleeCondition: If the player presents the coded letter or the payment receipt directly to him in conversation.
aggressionProfile.heavyPressure: Becomes stiff and formal, invoking his contributions to the cause: 'I have given money and time and risk to this movement. I will not stand here and be slandered.'
aggressionProfile.capturedBehavior: Collapses into bargaining — offers to name other Crown informants in exchange for safe passage out of Boston.
